focus: Evaluate a stronger dependency-free boosted-tree LTR model before moving to neural reranking or external embedding models.
reason_started: GEN-020 coordinate-ascent LTR showed ranking signal and in-sample capacity but remained below refined PRF under cross-validation.
reason_ended: Boosted-tree LTR improved graded cross-validation but did not beat refined PRF and regressed on binary cross-validation.

validators_executed.name: node --test test/ltr.test.js
validators_executed.evidence: Validated coordinate-ascent and boosted-tree LTR paths.

validators_executed.name: npm run eval:cranfield:ltr -- --model boosted-trees
validators_executed.evidence: Quick and depth-3 boosted-tree runs completed for graded nDCG@10 and binary nDCG@20.

validators_executed.name: npm run validate
validators_executed.evidence: 41 node tests passed; MDE parser, traceability validation through GEN-021, fixture index check, and fixture evaluation passed. Final run is recorded in VAL-031.

validators_skipped.name: remote OpenSearch LTR plugin validation
validators_skipped.reason: Deferred because offline cross-validated boosted-tree LTR did not beat refined PRF.

validators_skipped.name: Cloudflare deploy verification
validators_skipped.reason: No deployment was requested; GEN-021 leaves the public/default architecture unchanged.

evidence_quality_notes: Boosted-tree LTR improves graded held-out metrics versus coordinate-ascent LTR, but still remains below refined PRF.
Binary boosted-tree LTR is worse than the GEN-020 lexical LTR ablation.
In-sample boosted-tree metrics are high and reinforce the overfit risk; they are not promotion evidence.
